name: Starship (Cross-Shell Prompt)

performance.notes: Rust-based; async module evaluation; configurable timeout per module

agent_readiness.notes: Minimal, fast, infinitely customizable prompt for any shell. TOML config: ~/.config/starship.toml. Works with bash, zsh, fish, PowerShell, cmd, nushell. Modules: git status, language versions (Python, Node, Go, Rust, etc.), k8s context, AWS profile, duration, exit code. Nerd Font icons. Minimal preset, no installation overhead. vs Oh My Zsh (heavier, more plugins). vs Powerlevel10k (more features but zsh-only). Rust-based speed. 'eval $(starship init zsh)' setup.

gotchas: Shell tool only — not a library
TOML config has learning curve for complex customization
Slower than bare prompt if many modules enabled
best_when: Developers wanting consistent prompt across multiple shells/machines
Cross-platform teams (Windows PowerShell, macOS zsh, Linux bash)
Developers wanting language version, git, k8s context in prompt
Teams using dotfiles wanting portable prompt configuration
avoid_when: Teams using Oh My Zsh with established configuration
Environments without Nerd Font rendering (icons won't show)
summary: Starship is a minimal, fast, cross-shell prompt written in Rust. Works with bash, zsh, fish, PowerShell, nushell. TOML config with modules for git status, Python/Node/Go versions, k8s context, AWS profile. Nerd Font icons. Fast due to async evaluation. Best for developers wanting a consistent, informative prompt across multiple shells and machines. The modern alternative to Oh My Zsh prompts and Powerlevel10k for cross-shell use.
